evidences.title: This New Comedy Has Unexpectedly Pulled Past Masters of the Universe in Box Office Predictions
evidences.content: ## Scary Movie 6 has pulled ahead of Masters of the Universe in box office predictions A new box office prediction for Scary Movie 6 has the reboot earning $43 million to $53 million in its domestic opening weekend from June 5 to June 7, according to a May 22 long-range forecast from BoxOffice Pro. When tracking first started for the film on May 8, analytics site BoxOfficeTheory projected that its domestic opener would be much lower at around $25 million to $35 million. This means the movie's box office projections have almost doubled in just two weeks. While the latest May 22 report from BoxOfficeTheory isn't as bullish as BoxOffice Pro's, it has still increased the prediction for Scary Movie 6 to $41 million, in the range of $35 million to $52 million, for its domestic start. [...] [Image Credit: Paramount Pictures] Scary Movie 6 has surpassed Masters of the Universe in box office predictions, with a forecasted domestic opening of $43 million to $53 million. The reboot of Scary Movie 6, set to release on June 5, 2026, has seen a significant increase in interest since its tracking began, with the original cast returning after more than a decade. The success of Scary Movie 6's b...

evidences.title: Scary Movie (2026 film) - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Scary Movie (also known as Scary Movie 6) is a 2026 American parody film directed by Michael Tiddes and written by Marlon Wayans, Shawn Wayans, Keenen Ivory Wayans, Craig Wayans, and Rick Alvarez. It is the sixth installment in the Scary Movie film series "Scary Movie (film series)") and has been referred to as the spiritual sequel to the first two films. It stars Marlon, Shawn, Anna Faris, and Regina Hall. The plot follows Cindy Campbell and her friends Ray Wilkins and siblings Shorty and Brenda Meeks reunited when the same masked killer from the original film Scary Movie resurfaces. [...] | The film's cast are seen sitting in a cinema._poster.jpg "The film's cast are seen sitting in a cinema.") evidences.title: Long Range Forecast: Moviegoers Say Whazzzaaaappp to Rebooted SCARY MOVIE - Boxoffice Pro
evidences.content: A debut on the high end of our $43M – $53M opening weekend range puts Scary Movie in the ballpark of Final Destination: Bloodlines—not as big a box office champ as the rebooted Scream franchise, but something with a bit more impact than I Know What You Did Last Summer or 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple. A debut on the low end puts this new Scary Movie alongside the original Scary Movie, which had an opening weekend gross of $42.3M back in 2000. evidences.title: Scary Movie 6 (2026) - Anna Faris, Marlon Wayans
evidences.content: After 13 years away from the franchise, the most hilariously accident-prone survivors in horror parody history are officially back. Scary Movie 6 is confirmed and heading to theaters on June 5, 2026, distributed by Paramount Pictures and produced by Miramax, marking the first time the Wayans family has returned to write and produce the franchise since Scary Movie 2 back in 2001. Anna Faris and Regina Hall are officially confirmed to reprise their iconic roles as Cindy Campbell and Brenda Meeks, with Marlon Wayans back as Shorty Meeks and Shawn Wayans returning as Ray Wilkins. The full returning cast also includes Jon Abrahams, Lochlyn Munro, Dave Sheridan, Cheri Oteri, Chris Elliott, and Anthony Anderson, with new additions including Damon Wayans Jr., Heidi Gardner, Kim Wayans, and Sydney [...] Marlon, Shawn, and Keenen Ivory are all returning as screenwriters and producers, marking their first collaboration in 17 years since they walked away from the franchise. And the script is co-written by Marlon Wayans, Shawn Wayans, Keenen Ivory Wayans, Craig Wayans, and Rick Alvarez, which means the creative DNA that made the original two films so brilliant is completely restored and full...

Neal H. Moritz (“The Fast and the Furious”) is also a producer. “Scary Movie 6” is directed by Michael Tiddes, who previously directed several Wayans brothers films, including “A Haunted House” and Netflix’s “Naked” and “Sextuplets.” ↑ Back to top []( ## Where to find casting calls for “Scary Movie 6” [...] Content For:Actors + PerformersBrands + CreativesCastingCreatorsCrewVoiceover and Regina Hall (“Girls Trip,” “The Hate U Give”) are making a long-awaited return to their iconic roles after skipping the fifth installment in 2013. They’ll reunite with Shawn, Marlon, and Keenen Ivory Wayans, who launched the horror-comedy franchise and last worked on “Scary Movie 2” in 2001. The Wayans brothers will co-write and produce the new film, which is being developed by Miramax for Paramount, nearly 25 years after the original “Scary Movie” hit theaters in 2000. The return of creators The Wayans’ and original stars Marlon Wayans and Anna Faris rings the nostalgia bell big time. Cons [...] ### 3. Backrooms A24 | Week 2 Opening Weekend Range: $30M – $35M Showtime Market Share: 13% Pros Absolutely no one expected A24’s Backrooms to launch with $81.4M, even though it piggybacked on a YouTube phenomenon. Theaters scrambled to book more screens as pre-orders went through the roof in the days leading up to release, resulting in the biggest box office surprise of the year. Backrooms is coming in red hot when it comes to buzz this weekend, and we could potentially see the title begin to expand beyond its core audience base this weekend. evidences.title: Long Range Forecast: I KNOW WHAT YOU DID LAST SUMMER ...
evidences.content: Our forecasting puts Eddington roughly in line with fellow dark comedy (and fellow A24 wide release) Death of a Unicorn, which bowed to $5.7M in late March of this year. Other subject matter comps include A24 dark comedy Friendship and the Joaquin Phoenix-starring He Won’t Get Far On Foot—however, both of those films opened in only a handful of theaters before expanding to a larger footprint in chase weeks, rendering them unhelpful when it comes to debut forecasting. So far in 2025, A24 has gone straight to a wide domestic release with June’s Materialists ($11.3M) , April’s Warfare ($8.3M), May’s Bring Her Back ($7.1M), and the aforementioned March release Death of a Unicorn ($5.7M). ## Long Range Forecast — July 18, 2025 ## Smurfs | Paramount [...] Our forecasting panel’s $15M – $20M opening weekend range puts I Know What You Did Last Summer far below Final Destination: Bloodlines ($51.6M), January 2022’s Scream ($30M), March 2023’s Scream VI ($44.4M), and fellow summer 2025 horror sequel 28 Years Later ($30M). A slight debut weekend overperformance would put I Know What You Did Last Summer in the realm ofApril 2023’s Evil Dead Rise ($24.5M) or August 2022’s Candyman ($22M), wh...

evidences.title: The Biggest Movies Coming to Theaters in August 2025
evidences.content: Neon aims to replicate the summer horror success of last year’s _Longlegs_ with _Together_, a body horror love story directed and written by Michael Shanks, starring real-life couple Dave Franco and Alison Brie. Tim and Millie’s rural relocation spirals into a nightmarish descent when an unnatural force begins to warp their relationship—and their bodies. While most of the distributor’s horror releases have capped under $15M domestically, _Longlegs_ proved there’s real upside when the right film catches fire. Helping boost its profile is a clever marketing campaign, which has included the opportunity for couples attending opening weekend to score free therapy sessions via partner platform OurRitual. The campaign also offered one lucky couple the opportunity to win a Vegas wedding by [...] After the surprise success of _Barbarian_, director Zach Cregger is back with _Weapons_, a horror-thriller that blends true-crime elements with supernatural terror. With a chilling premise—the disappearance of all members of an elementary school class except one—_Weapons_ taps into a familiar fear of the unknown and the menacing forces lurking beneath the surface of everyday life. The film stars...

evidences.title: The Biggest Movies Coming to Theaters in February 2025
evidences.content: ## _\_Heart Eyes\__| Sony ### February 7 Who says romance is dead? According to the synopsis of Screen Gems’ _Heart Eyes_, for the past several years the “Heart Eyes Killer” has been wreaking havoc on Valentine’s Day by stalking and murdering couples in the mood for love. The masked psychopath only strikes on Valentine’s Day, proving a solid crossover of horror and rom com elements. Actor, writer, and director Josh Ruben helms. Ruben is known for creating horror/comedy mashups such as the Sundance film _Scare Me_ and the 2021 video game adaptation _Werewolves Within_ ($575K domestic/$991K global). Olivia Holt, Mason Gooding, and Jordana Brewster star. Sony Pictures distributes via Screen Gems. Last year’s Screen Gems horror offering _Tarot_ generated $18.7M domestic and $49.2M global. [...] The month of February arrives with the promise of a little breathing room in the 2025 calendar, allowing titles time to find their an audiences in advance of much closer quarters later this spring and beyond. The biggest hopeful of the month is Disney’s return to the MCU, _Captain America: Brave New World_ (Feb. 14th), a Presidents’ Day weekend entry that aims to recapture the political intri...
